number-systems.q: Which of the following is NOT a Natural number?
number-systems.exp: Natural numbers start from 1. Zero is a Whole number, but not a Natural number.
Any number that can be expressed as a fraction $$ \frac{p}{q} $$ is a Rational number.

number-systems.q: Which of these is an Irrational number?
number-systems.exp: $$ \sqrt{9} = 3 $$ (Rational). $$ 3.14 $$ and $$ \frac{22}{7} $$ are rational fractions. $$ \sqrt{5} $$ cannot be simplified to a fraction, so it is Irrational.

number-systems.q: Is the number $$ -5 $$ a Rational number?
number-systems.exp: Yes. Every integer is a rational number because it can be written as a fraction over 1 (e.g. $$ \frac{-5}{1} $$).

number-systems.q: What set does the number $$ 0.3333... $$ belong to?
number-systems.exp: Repeating decimals are Rational numbers because they can be converted to fractions. $$ 0.333... = \frac{1}{3} $$.

number-systems.q: True or False: Every Real number is either Rational or Irrational.
number-systems.exp: True. The set of Real numbers ($$ \mathbb{R} $$) is the union of Rational ($$ \mathbb{Q} $$) and Irrational ($$ \mathbb{Q}' $$) numbers.

real-number-system.q: Which property is shown here: $$ a + b = b + a $$?
real-number-system.exp: Commutative comes from 'commute' (to move around). The order doesn't change the sum.
Absolute value is distance from zero, which is always positive.
The bracket '[' means inclusive, and the parenthesis ')' means exclusive.
Absolute value is a distance, so it can NEVER be negative. Thus, there is no real number that satisfies this.
The number 3 is 'distributed' or multiplied to both terms inside the parentheses.

real-number-system.q: How would you write 'all real numbers strictly greater than 5' in interval notation?
real-number-system.exp: Strictly greater means exclude 5 (use parentheses). Infinity ALWAYS uses parentheses.

logarithms.q: Rewrite the exponent equation as a logarithm: $$ 3^4 = 81 $$
logarithms.exp: The base is 3, the exponent is 4, and the result is 81. So, $$ \log_3(81) = 4 $$.
Ask yourself: 10 raised to what power is 1000? $$ 10^3 = 1000 $$, so the answer is 3.
The Product Rule states that the log of a product is the sum of the logs: $$ \log(a) + \log(b) $$.
The exponent '5' moves to the front as a multiplier: $$ 5 \log_2(x) $$.
Any base raised to the power of 0 equals 1. So, the log of 1 is always 0.

logarithms.q: Which of the following is equivalent to $$ \log_e(x) $$?
logarithms.exp: The natural logarithm (base $e$) is written as $$ \ln(x) $$.

sets.exp: Intersection ($ \cap $) means what they have in common. They both share 5 and 9.
Union ($ \cup $) combines them. We do not repeat elements, so it's $$ \{2, 4, 6\} $$.

sets.q: Which statement is true if $$ C = \{3, 4, 5\} $$?
sets.exp: $$ 4 \in C $$ means 4 is an element of C. A number itself is an element ($ \in $), while a set with a number is a subset ($ \subset $).
$$ X - Y $$ means start with X and remove anything that is also in Y. We remove 3. We are left with $$ \{1, 2\} $$.
$$ \emptyset $$ represents the Empty Set, a set containing absolutely nothing.

relations-functions.exp: No, because the input 1 maps to both 2 and 5.

relations-functions.q: What is the domain of $$ f(x) = \frac{1}{x-3} $$?
relations-functions.exp: The denominator cannot be zero. Therefore, $$ x - 3 \neq 0 \implies x \neq 3 $$.

relations-functions.q: A graph passes the Vertical Line Test. What does this mean?
opts: It is a relation but not a function
relations-functions.exp: Passing the test means no vertical line crosses the graph more than once, ensuring one output per input.

binary-operations.q: What is the identity element for standard addition?
binary-operations.exp: $$ a + 0 = a $$. So 0 is the additive identity.

binary-operations.q: A binary operation $*$ on set $\mathbb{R}$ is defined as $$ a * b = a + b + ab $$. Find $$ 2 * 3 $$.

logic.q: If P is True and Q is False, what is the value of $$ P \land Q $$?
logic.exp: AND ($ \land $) requires both to be True.

logic.q: When is the implication $$ P \implies Q $$ FALSE?
opts: When P is F and Q is F
When P is T and Q is T
When P is T and Q is F
When P is F and Q is T
logic.exp: An implication is only broken if the premise (P) happens but the promise (Q) does not.
Double negation brings you back to the original value.

combinatorics.q: You have 4 colors of paint and 3 types of brushes. How many unique paint-brush combinations can you make?
You can travel to the city by taking 1 of 5 trains OR 1 of 3 buses. How many ways can you travel?
If there are 367 people in a room, what does the Pigeonhole Principle guarantee?
opts: At least two people share the same birthday
Someone was born on a leap year
combinatorics.exp: There are only 366 possible birthdays (including leap year). With 367 people, at least 2 must share a birthday.

permutations-combinations.exp: By mathematical convention, the factorial of zero is 1.

permutations-combinations.q: Selecting 3 people from a group of 10 to form a committee. Is this a permutation or combination?
permutations-combinations.exp: Order does not matter in a committee. A,B,C is the same committee as B,C,A.

permutations-combinations.q: Entering a 4-digit PIN code. Is this a permutation or combination?
permutations-combinations.exp: Order absolutely matters. 1234 is not the same PIN as 4321.

induction.q: What is the purpose of the Base Case?
opts: To prove the formula works for every number
To prove the formula works for the first number
To assume it works for k
induction.exp: The base case (usually n=1) establishes the starting point. It's knocking over the first domino.

induction.q: In the Inductive Step, what do we assume to be true?
induction.exp: We assume the statement holds for an arbitrary integer k, written as $$ P(k) $$.

induction.q: Mathematical Induction is used to prove statements for which set of numbers?
induction.exp: Induction operates in discrete steps (1, 2, 3...) so it applies to Natural (or Whole) numbers.

sequence-series.exp: Each term is multiplied by 2. That makes it a Geometric Progression (GP).

sequence-series.q: In the AP $$ 2, 5, 8, 11... $$, what is the common difference (d)?
sequence-series.exp: Take any term and subtract the previous one: $$ 5 - 2 = 3 $$.

sequence-series.q: What is the sum to infinity of a GP with $$ a=1 $$ and $$ r=\frac{1}{2} $$?

trigonometry.exp: Tangent is Sine divided by Cosine.
This is the fundamental Pythagorean identity. It always equals 1.

trigonometry.q: What is the reciprocal of Cosine ($$ \cos $$)?

complex.q: What is the value of $$ i^2 $$?
complex.exp: By definition, $$ i = \sqrt{-1} $$, so squaring it gives -1.

complex.q: What is the complex conjugate of $$ 5 - 2i $$?
complex.exp: To find the conjugate, flip the sign of the imaginary part.

equations.q: If the discriminant $$ b^2 - 4ac $$ is negative, what does this mean?
equations.exp: A negative discriminant means you are taking the square root of a negative number, which results in imaginary/complex roots.

inequalities.exp: Divide by -3. Because we divided by a negative number, flip the sign: $$ x \le -4 $$.

inequalities.q: How is $$ x < 7 $$ represented on a number line?
inequalities.exp: Less than means an open circle (not included) pointing left (smaller numbers).
Strict less than gets a parenthesis `(`. Less than or equal to gets a bracket `]`.

matrices.q: What is the dimension of a matrix with 3 rows and 2 columns?
matrices.exp: Dimensions are always stated as Rows by Columns (R x C).

matrices.q: Can you add a $$ 2 \times 3 $$ matrix and a $$ 3 \times 2 $$ matrix?
matrices.exp: No. Matrices must have the exact same dimensions to be added or subtracted.

matrices.q: If A is a $$ 2 \times 3 $$ matrix and B is a $$ 3 \times 4 $$ matrix, what is the dimension of AB?
matrices.exp: Inner dimensions must match (3=3). Outer dimensions give the size of the new matrix: $$ 2 \times 4 $$.

determinants.q: If the determinant of a matrix is 0, what do we call the matrix?
determinants.exp: A matrix with a determinant of 0 is called singular, meaning it cannot be inverted.

determinants.q: What is the determinant of the Identity matrix $$ I_2 $$?
determinants.exp: The determinant of any Identity matrix is 1. It preserves area perfectly.

matrices-determinants-app.q: If the determinant of the coefficient matrix $$ |A| = 0 $$, what does it mean for the system of equations?
matrices-determinants-app.exp: If |A| = 0, the matrix is singular. Therefore, it has either no solutions or infinitely many solutions, but NOT a unique one.

matrices-determinants-app.q: In the matrix equation $$ AX = B $$, what does X represent?
matrices-determinants-app.exp: X is the column matrix of the variables we are trying to solve for.

vectors.q: If the dot product of two non-zero vectors is 0, what does that mean?
vectors.exp: Since $$ \cos(90^\circ) = 0 $$, a dot product of 0 means the angle between them is 90 degrees.

vectors.q: What kind of result does the Cross Product produce?
vectors.exp: The cross product produces a new vector that is orthogonal to the original two.

3d-geometry.q: What is the distance from the origin (0,0,0) to the point (2,3,6)?
In the equation of a plane $$ 3x + 4y - 2z = 10 $$, what is the normal vector?
3d-geometry.exp: The coefficients of x, y, and z form the normal vector: $$ \langle 3, 4, -2 \rangle $$.

diff-app.q: To find the critical points of a function, you must:
opts: Set the first derivative to zero
Set the second derivative to zero
diff-app.exp: Critical points occur where the slope (first derivative) is zero.

diff-app.q: If the second derivative at a critical point is positive, what type of point is it?
diff-app.exp: A positive second derivative means the curve is concave UP (like a smile), indicating a local minimum.

integration.q: What is the integral of $$ f(x) = 3x^2 $$?
integration.exp: Using the power rule: add 1 to power (3), divide by new power (3). $$ \frac{3x^3}{3} = x^3 + C $$.

integration.q: Why do we add $$ + C $$ to indefinite integrals?
opts: To account for any constant that was lost during differentiation
integration.exp: The derivative of any constant is 0. So when integrating backwards, we add C to represent that unknown constant.

int-app.q: To find the area between $$ y = x^2 $$ and the x-axis from x=0 to x=2, you evaluate:
int-app.exp: Just set up the definite integral of the function bounded by 0 and 2.

int-app.q: What physical quantity do you get when you integrate acceleration?
int-app.exp: Acceleration is the derivative of velocity. So integrating acceleration gives you velocity back.

linear-diff-equations.q: What is the integrating factor for $$ \frac{dy}{dx} + 2y = e^x $$?
linear-diff-equations.exp: $$ P(x) = 2 $$. Integrating factor $$ = e^{\int 2 dx} = e^{2x} $$.
A DE asks 'What function makes this relationship true?' So the answer is a function.

exact-equations.q: What makes the equation $$ M(x,y)dx + N(x,y)dy = 0 $$ exact?
exact-equations.exp: The mixed partial derivatives must be equal.

diff-equations-app.q: In the exponential growth model $$ P(t) = P_0 e^{kt} $$, what does $$ P_0 $$ represent?
diff-equations-app.exp: When t=0, $$ e^0 = 1 $$, so $$ P(0) = P_0 $$, the starting population.

diff-equations-app.q: Newton's Law of Cooling implies that a hot cup of coffee will cool down:
opts: Faster initially, then slower as it nears room temperature
diff-equations-app.exp: The rate of cooling is proportional to the temperature difference. As it cools, the difference shrinks, so the rate slows down.

linear-programming.q: In a linear programming problem, where does the optimal solution occur?
opts: In the middle of the feasible region
At one of the corner points of the feasible region
linear-programming.exp: The Corner Point Theorem states that the max or min of the objective function will always occur at a vertex of the feasible region.

linear-programming.q: What does the inequality $$ x \ge 0, y \ge 0 $$ mean graphically?
opts: The graph is only in the first quadrant
The graph is a straight line
linear-programming.exp: These non-negativity constraints restrict our graph to the upper-right (first) quadrant, representing real-world positive quantities.

statistics.q: What is the median of the dataset: 2, 4, 10, 14, 20?
statistics.exp: The median is the exact middle value of an ordered set. Here, it is 10.

statistics.q: If the variance of a dataset is 25, what is the standard deviation?
statistics.exp: Standard deviation is the square root of the variance: $$ \sqrt{25} = 5 $$.

statistics.q: Which measure is most affected by extreme outliers?
statistics.exp: The mean includes every value in its sum, so a massive outlier will heavily skew the average.

correlation-regression.q: What does a correlation coefficient of $$ r = 0.95 $$ indicate?
correlation-regression.exp: An r value close to 1 means the variables strongly move up together.

correlation-regression.q: What is the purpose of linear regression?
opts: To find the best fitting line for prediction
To measure the spread of data
correlation-regression.exp: Regression draws a line of best fit through the scatter plot, allowing us to predict y values for new x values.

probability.q: What is the probability of rolling an even number on a standard 6-sided die?
probability.exp: There are 3 even numbers (2,4,6) out of 6 total possibilities. 3/6 simplifies to 1/2.

probability.q: If the probability of it raining tomorrow is 0.3, what is the probability of it NOT raining?

discrete-prob-dist.q: Which distribution would you use to model the number of customer arrivals at a bank in a 1-hour window?
discrete-prob-dist.exp: The Poisson distribution is specifically designed for counting the number of events in a fixed interval of time.

discrete-prob-dist.q: In a Binomial Distribution, what does 'n' stand for?
discrete-prob-dist.exp: 'n' is the fixed number of trials, like tossing a coin 10 times.

statistical-estimation.q: What does a 95% confidence interval mean?
opts: 95% of the data falls in this interval
We are 95% confident the true population mean falls in this interval
There is a 5% chance the math is wrong
statistical-estimation.exp: It means if we repeated the survey many times, 95% of the intervals generated would contain the true population mean.

statistical-estimation.q: To decrease the margin of error in a confidence interval, what should you do?
statistical-estimation.exp: A larger sample size (n) shrinks the standard error, making your estimate more precise.

differential-geometry.q: What does the curvature ($$\kappa$$) of a straight line equal?
differential-geometry.exp: A straight line does not bend, so its curvature is zero.

differential-geometry.q: Which property measures how much a curve twists out of a 2D plane?
differential-geometry.exp: Torsion measures the 'twist' of a space curve.
